subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: save time by generating boilerplate code

subcommands.name: cell
subcommands.description: generate a cell component

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
use this flag to generate a list cell. This flag is needed when dealing with irregular words whose plural and singular is identical such as equipment or pokemon

subcommands.description: generate a component component

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.description: generate a data migration component

subcommands.description: generate a directive

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.name: function

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

generate a layout with a skip link [default: false]

subcommands.name: page

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

states.name: path

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: generate Pages, SDL, and Services files based on a given DB schema Model. Also accepts <path/model>

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

states.name: model
states.description: model to scaffold. You can also use <path/model> to nest files by type at the given path directory (or directories). For example, redwood g scaffold admin/post

subcommands.name: sdl
subcommands.description: generate a GraphQL schema and service object

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

states.name: model
states.description: model to generate the sdl for

subcommands.name: service
subcommands.description: generate a service component

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.name: types
subcommands.description: generate types and supplementary code

subcommands.name: script
subcommands.description: generate a script that can use your services/libs to execute with redwood exec script <name>

options.description: generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: execute scripts generated by yarn redwood generate script to run one-off operations, long-running jobs, or utility scripts

states.name: script
states.description: name of the script to execute

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: build the api and web

options.description: use Webpack Bundle Analyzer to visualize the size of Webpack output files via an interactive zoomable treemap

subcommands.description: get structural diagnostics for a Redwood project (experimental)

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: launch an interactive Redwood shell (experimental)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: print your system environment information

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: upgrade all @redwoodjs packages via an interactive CLI

options.description: check for outdated packages without upgrading
choices are "canary", "rc", or a specific version (e.g. "0.19.3"). WARNING: Unstable releases in the case of "canary" and "rc", which will force upgrade package

values.description: Upgrade to the latest canary release
Upgrade to the latest RC release

options.description: installs packages for the given PR

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: runs a server that serves both the api and the web sides

options.description: what port should the server run on [default: 8911]

value.name: port
value.description: Port to run the server on

options.description: the socket the server should run. This takes precedence over port

value.name: socket
value.description: Socket to run the server on

options.description: the root path where your api functions are served
forwards requests from the apiProxyPath (defined in redwood.toml) to the specified host

states.name: side
states.description: which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: type-check
subcommands.description: runs a TypeScript compiler check on both the api and the web sides

states.name: side
states.description: which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: run Jest tests for api and web

options.description: run tests related to changed files based on hg/git (uncommitted files). Specify the name or path to a file to focus on a specific set of tests [default: true]
show test coverage summary and output info to coverage directory in project root. See this directory for an .html coverage report
delete the Jest cache directory and exit without running tests
syncs the test database with your Prisma schema without requiring a migration. It creates a test database if it doesn't already exist [default: true]. This flag

states.name: sides-or-filter
states.description: which side(s) to test, and/or a regular expression to match against your test files to filter by
